mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb{T} be a geometric theory and let U_{ \mathbb{T} } be its generic model in \mathbf{Set} { \left [ \mathbb{T} \right ]}. The spectrum of a U_{ \mathbb{T} }-algebra M is the set of U_{ \mathbb{T} }-algebra homomorphisms M \to U_{ \mathbb{T} }. (Recall that a U_{ \mathbb{T} }-algebra is a model of \mathbb{T} equipped with a homomorphism from U_{ \mathbb{T} }.)

Let \mathbb{T} be a horn theory. The generic \mathbb{T}-model U_{ \mathbb{T} } is quasicoherent in the following sense: internal to \mathbf{Set} { \left [ \mathbb{T} \right ]}, for any finitely presentable U_{ \mathbb{T} }-algebra A, the canonical U_{ \mathbb{T} }-algebra homomorphism A \to U_{ \mathbb{T} } ^{ \operatorname{Spec} {A}} sending a to \lambda f: \operatorname{Spec} {A}. f \, a is an isomorphism, where \operatorname{Spec} {A} is the synthetic spectrum of A.

frontmatter.title: A general Nullstellensatz for generalized spaces

mainmatter.p: We give a general Nullstellensatz for the generic model of a geometric theory, useful as a source of nongeometric sequents validated by the generic model, and characterize the first-order and higher-order formulas validated by the generic model.
